LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APR-07-2022 HEARING RE: RESPONDENT DR. SHIRLEY N. WEBER, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY'S MOTION TO DISMISS FIRST AMENDED VERIFIED PETITION FOR ORDER DIRECTING PAYMENT OUT OF THE VICTIMS OF CORPORATE FRAUD COMPENSATION FUND, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION GRANTED WITH PREJUDICE. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Dismiss First Amended Verified Petition For Order Directing Payment Out Of The Victims Of Corporate Fraud Compensation Fund
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT DR. SHIRLEY N. WEBER, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY'S Motion To Dismiss First Amended Verified Petition For Order Directing Payment Out Of The Victims Of Corporate Fraud Compensation Fund.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Respondent Dr. Shirley N. Weber, in her official capacity as California Secretary of State's motion to dismiss Petitioner's First Amended Verified Petition for Order Directing Payment Out of the Victims of Corporate Fraud Compensation Fund ("VCFCF Act"), is granted with prejudice.

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
